mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS reflects continued monetary tightening, raising the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and compressing valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days whenever the rate trend is upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rising-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals a turning point in the labor cycle that historically precedes broader earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns following triggered UNRATE increases are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST housing starts and an elevated or rising FEDFUNDS level jointly indicate that tight monetary policy is suppressing interest-rate-sensitive demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as the housing-credit channel transmits stress to broader equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains above its 12-month moving average
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing slowdown
failure_condition: Joint HOUST decline and elevated FEDFUNDS signal fails to produce statistically significant negative 60-day returns relative to unconditional baseline (t-test p>0.05) across at least two historical tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration in the 3-month annualized growth rate of INDPRO signals waning industrial momentum ahead of a broader slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth deceleration signal fails to precede below-median 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of late-cycle instances over the historical record.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market expansion and consumer income support, generating short-horizon positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as investors extrapolate near-term earnings resilience.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 5-day SPX returns is below 0.02 across rolling 2-year windows, indicating no exploitable short-term momentum relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS reaches or exceeds a cycle-high threshold consistent with the terminal rate of a tightening campaign, markets begin pricing an approaching pause, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as discount-rate expectations stabilize.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS reaches new 24-month high and remains unchanged for 2 consecutive policy meetings
regime_hint: late-stage monetary tightening cycle nearing pause
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns following FEDFUNDS plateau signals are not significantly positive (p>0.05) or occur less than 55% of the time across historical tightening-cycle endings.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously rising and GDPC1 growth is decelerating, the joint pattern signals stagflation risk that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and rising, FEDFUNDS rising, and GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 2%
failure_condition: Joint trigger fails to produce significantly negative 60-day SPX returns relative to baseline (p>0.05) in at least two of the last three historical occurrences of the combined pattern.

mechanism_narrative: In a consumer-driven expansion regime, month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS retail sales signals robust near-term consumption strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as analysts revise consumer-sector earnings estimates upward.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month acceleration shows Rank IC below 0.02 with forward 5-day SPX returns over rolling 2-year windows, or effect reverses sign in non-recessionary periods.
